generated_by: glyphs/knoll.py — do not edit registry.json or the viewer data block by hand; re-run the generator
discipline: every entry carries a provenance status: PINNED (quoted, source cited) | DERIVED (stated rule from pins) | MINTED (coined, provisional) | OPEN (named hole)

polysheaf.name: Polysheaf ternary lettering
polysheaf.rule: corners A–H, edges I–T, faces U–Z; edges grouped by open axis (x, then y, then z), faces by their normal axis; within a group, natural sign order (− < 0 < +) on the remaining coordinates; the all-zeros center is the obscured wildcard ⊘, letterless

cells.note: the obscured wildcard — anchored by no pin, requests nothing; the null/generative slot where the cascade terminates

cube_alphabet.name: Cube-alphabet piece lettering
cube_alphabet.names_what: occupants — mobile pieces; coords are home (solved) slots under x:L−/R+, y:D−/U+, z:B−/F+ (a derived convention)
cube_alphabet.rule: piece type (centers, edges, corners), then standard face order U D L R F B

cells.note: not a piece — the mechanism is a void with an axis through it; what enables rotation is the absence of a cubie

terms.referent: The unit; computational-presence atom; pluralizes to *onta
terms.non_example: A passive storage byte

terms.referent: Plural of ontum; scale-recursively, the constituents that settle into an ontum
terms.non_example: A single ontum viewed alone

terms.referent: The center cell / Token of an ontum; frame-relative, correction-bearing
terms.non_example: An inert unweighted centroid (a dead token)

terms.referent: Per-keystone property setting *how* a correction is admitted; makes the center an operator
terms.non_example: A global constant applied to all keystones alike

terms.referent: The process by which the cells' collective pull translates the keystone
terms.non_example: A center that sits deaf while its cells pull

terms.referent: Relation between finite spec and infinite output, layers untouching
terms.non_example: A large-but-bounded set

terms.referent: An ontum carrying its generator-lineage as type
terms.non_example: A token with erased `derived_from`

terms.referent: The keystone accepting its correction at one moment, read in a fixed frame
terms.non_example: Barycenter coincident with the keystone

terms.referent: The trajectory of the cant over updates
terms.non_example: A single static correction

terms.referent: Substrate where every ontum traces its root
terms.non_example: Any substrate with one provenance-silent ontum

terms.referent: grip = names a referent; hole = names a slot; pin = attaches a non-example
terms.non_example: A definition with no falsifier (still a hole)

terms.referent: Former unit-name
Former provenance-token name; never defined its epistemic payload
Static finite-primary/infinite-secondary structure (name collision)
Self-issuing projection frame (issued by the projected, not the observer); may be the *kind* of -jection a jective admits
terms.non_example: A projection with an external observer role

terms.referent: A primitive treated as a local frame; holds its local stalk (with dynamics), an inward encoding, axis/antipode structure, and outgoing maps. Simultaneously a whole and a part.
terms.non_example: A global object authored as its own file

terms.referent: A primitive that is a join. Connects Sites and carries the comparison between them as a cocycle. A Seam that does not close is not a bug; it is data.
terms.non_example: A boolean joined/not-joined flag

terms.referent: Not authored. The emergent index: the collection of Sites and Seams plus their incidence. The whole is read off the Atlas, never written as its own file.
terms.non_example: A hand-maintained global map

terms.referent: Self-casting (thrown-by-itself): a structure that generates its own slots and the constraint that shapes what may fill them.
terms.non_example: A projection with an external observer role

terms.referent: The 27th grid position, dead center: not a piece. A void with an axis through it — what enables rotation is the absence of a cubie.
terms.non_example: A 27th cubie

terms.referent: Symbol systems work because their center is empty. The mechanism is the negative space. Structure rotates around an absence.
terms.non_example: A token system whose semantics live inside one privileged symbol

terms.referent: A typed form whose source, structure, and downstream effects are all inspectable — the atomic unit of trust.
terms.non_example: A generated artifact whose formation chain died in the conversation

terms.referent: The gating mechanism: certifies (never generates) ontograms; six checks, three verdicts — admit, refuse, instrumentation-needed.
terms.non_example: A generator that grades its own output

trio.note: Three candidate readings, knolled side by side. A grip needs a non-example; until bdo pins one reading (or mints a fourth), the trio is a named hole, not a referent.

readings.gloss: One seam per axis — I on x, O on y, S on z — and all three share the corner E = (+,−,−): S·I·O is exactly the request set of one Self, E's seam-star. (The worked example's corner A requests I, M, Q; the corner that requests I, O, S is E.) A seam basis for the compression frame, issued by a single point.

reading.S: corner UFR — the first corner, the canonical home piece

readings.gloss: S leads the corner octave (S–Z); I and O are edges. All three carry state — none is a stateless anchor.

reading.O: the ring: a stroke enclosing a void — the empty center made visible; the spindle/wildcard drawn as a glyph
reading.I: the stroke: one decided axis — a line, the minimal edge; the unit presence
reading.S: the seam: one curve crossing between two lobes — the join drawn as a glyph; also the initial of Site, Seam, Self, settle, spindle

readings.gloss: I/O is additionally the binary pair — 1 and 0, two stickers around a void (cube-alphabet §5) — and S is the path between them. Ontology (O), instance (I), seam (S): the fabric, a fiber, and the stitch.

core27.name: Core 27
core27.coined_by: bdo — 'one term of those 27 glyphs ends up being the S/Void term for each letter; these are like our CORE 27 operational terms' (glyph-knolling session 0009)
core27.principle: Every glyph of the 27 is the Self/void term of its own local frame: globally a cell among cells, locally the keystone occupying its frame's empty center. The 27 glyphs are therefore the system's core operational terms — 26 letters and ⊘, each one anchoring a neighborhood. Globally there is one void; locally, every glyph voids its own center.
core27.gradient: openness by kind — corner frames hold 7 neighbors (19 slots open), edges 11/15, faces 17/9, and ⊘ 26/0: the root's frame is the whole specimen and closes completely.
core27.non_example: A grip-ledger word without an address — cant, drift, jective: operational vocabulary, but not core-27; they have no cell and anchor no local frame.

terms.note: the root: zero open slots — its local frame is the entire specimen

term_frames.contract: v0 — center: the Self/void term (the glyph). faces: directional relations derived from coordinates (seams decide, requests open, out-of-bounds directions carry the cell's own decisions). corners: settled facts in lex slot order (kind, dim, codim, axis, status, frame capacity, wing, home piece). edges: relations in queue order (antipode, live occupant, trio membership); unfilled edge slots stay OPEN — capacity for future relationships. Derived in knoll.py; re-arranging is re-knolling.

findings.observation: The same 26 letters are assigned twice, incompatibly: cube-alphabet gives A = U center / S = corner UFR; the polysheaf gives A = corner (−,−,−) / S = a z-edge. I and O are edges in both; A and S change kind entirely.
findings.resolution: Read as the (position, value) split both docs already name (cube-alphabet §3): polysheaf letters name fixed CELLS (addresses), cube-alphabet letters name mobile PIECES (occupants). One alphabet for where, one for what. A full state is a pairing of the two; the solved cube is the canonical pairing. Held MINTED until bdo admits or refuses the reading.

findings.observation: Line 111 says 'the count of zeros in a cell's coordinate is its codimension', but line 115 calls corner (−,−,−) — zero zeros — 'codim 3'. Count-of-zeros is the cell's DIMENSION (open axes); codimension is 3 minus that (decided axes). One of the two lines is mis-worded.
findings.resolution: Vault is read-only for this stream: reported, not patched. The registry records both numbers per cell (dim = zeros, codim = decided) so either fix leaves the data correct.

findings.observation: Both letterings leave (0,0,0) letterless, for different reasons: the spindle ∅ is the mechanism void (absence enables rotation); the obscured wildcard ⊘ is the generative null (requests nothing, terminates the cascade). Two readings of one empty center — the system's load-bearing absence.
findings.resolution: No action; knolled here so the alignment stays visible.
